title: ranunculus propinquilobus
intro: Ranunculus propinquilobus, also known as lesser caltha or marsh buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antitumor effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus propinquilobus include its potential in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
